title: salix × irreflexa
intro: Salix × irreflexa is a hybrid willow tree, derived from the cross between Salix purpurea and Salix × sepulcralis. The bark and leaves of this plant have been traditionally used in medicine, containing salicylic acid, which is a compound similar to aspirin and provides anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The medicinal properties of Salix × irreflexa have been used to treat various health issues, including pain relief, fever reduction, and inflammation, as well as to aid in wound healing and improve digestive health. Salix × irreflexa can be prepared as a herbal remedy through infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is often used to create willow bark tea or supplements for its medicinal benefits.
